_readme: PROPOSED corrections applied at BUILD TIME on top of data/current/dictionary.xlsx. The source spreadsheet is NEVER modified — these are hypotheses pending the professor's review. Once the professor folds a correction into a future snapshot, delete it from here. See data/CORRECTIONS.md for the human-readable rationale. Each correction records how many entries it affects so high-impact fixes are distinguishable from trivial ones.
_acceptedErrorBudgetNote: The build fails if the number of UNRESOLVED data errors (after corrections) exceeds this. It absorbs a small long-tail of genuine one-off data issues that need the professor, while still failing loudly if a snapshot regresses badly (e.g. a shifted column producing hundreds of errors). All known errors are currently corrected (0 remaining); this headroom absorbs new one-offs in future snapshots. Lower toward 0 as the data stabilizes.

gramAbbreviationAdditions.rationale: Used as Word class 2 on ~170 verb entries but absent from the abbrs-gram sheet. INTR is the standard grammatical gloss for an intransitive verb; the sheet already defines the parallel category set for verbs, so this is a missing row rather than an ambiguous code.

gramAbbreviationAliases.rationale: ~7 GLOSSARY rows reference 'INDECLold', but abbrs-gram defines it as 'INDECL-old' (indeclinable-old). Missing-hyphen typo; aliased to reuse the professor's own label.
GLOSSARY row 5012 uses 'INDEF', but abbrs-gram defines the indefinite-pronoun code as 'INDF'. Aliased to the existing definition.

paradigmKeyAliases.rationale: ~175 GLOSSARY rows reference paradigm key 'NME-i', but the M-DECL header cell spells it 'NM-E-i'. Every sibling masculine key uses the un-hyphenated 'NME-' prefix (NME-o-1, NME-A, NME-u, NME-∅-1, ...), so the single M-DECL header cell is the outlier typo and 'NME-i' is the intended key. Aliasing lets those 175 entries render their declension correctly.
GLOSSARY row 2397 (Dél) references 'NME-IRR-01', but M-DECL's irregular masculine keys use the 'NM-IRR-' prefix. Verified: NM-IRR-01 in M-DECL is the Dél/Devle(s) paradigm.
GLOSSARY row 2467 (dí) references 'NME-IRR-02'; M-DECL's NM-IRR-02 is the dí/díje(s) paradigm.
GLOSSARY row 10730 (srásta) references 'NME-IRR-03'; M-DECL's NM-IRR-03 is the srásta paradigm.
GLOSSARY row 4211 (grá) references 'NME-IRR-04'; M-DECL's NM-IRR-04 is the grá/gráste(s) paradigm.
GLOSSARY row 12217 (vá) references 'NME-IRR-05'; M-DECL's NM-IRR-05 is the vá/váste(s) paradigm.
GLOSSARY row 6869 (másek) references 'NFPE-∅-01', but F-DECL spells the key 'NFPE-∅-1' (single digit). Zero-padding typo.

glossaryCellOverrides.rationale: The Word class 1 cell for the entry 'briater-i/-kíja' (Brüter/in / 'breeder') contains 'N+V1536N1536:U1536', a leaked spreadsheet formula/cell-range fragment rather than a grammatical code. The entry is a noun (M/F flexion, NMF-02 nominal paradigm, noun glosses), so the intended word class is 'N'. Overriding the single cell keeps the real word in the dictionary instead of dropping it.
